commands.review_summary:
The branch moves Android notification app picker helpers into shared UI code, adds an App Filter picker to the current Notifications settings screen, and expands picker filtering unit tests.

PR surface: Other +230. Total +230 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main's Notifications settings route exposes mode and selected-app count but no picker path, while the legacy settings sheet still has package picker behavior. I did not run an emulator in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no narrow actionable defect was found; the open draft PR needs normal maintainer and CI handling.

target.title: fix(android): add notification app picker
target.body:
## Summary

- Restores a visible notification app picker in the current Android notification settings screen.
- Lets users search installed apps, include system packages when needed, and explicitly toggle selected packages for Blocklist or Allowlist mode.
- Moves the shared notification package query helpers out of the legacy settings sheet so both the current screen and tests use the same picker model.
- Keeps notification forwarding semantics unchanged: selection only updates `notifications.forwarding.packages`; it does not auto-enable forwarding or change listener/permission flow.
- AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the code path, kept the change scoped, and ran the validation below.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related none found. I checked the open Android-labeled PRs/issues before opening this PR and did not find a direct overlap for the notification app picker.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This is a narrow UI regression fix: the notification settings exposed Blocklist/Allowlist mode and a selected-app count, but the current settings screen did not expose a way to choose apps.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Android `Settings > Notifications` now exposes an `App Filter` panel with `Open App Picker`, app search, `Show System Apps`, app rows, and switches that persist selected package names.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Android third-party debug build on a local API 35 emulator.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Installed the third-party debug APK from this branch.
  - Opened `Settings > Notifications` in the app.
  - Expanded `App Filter` with `Open App Picker`.
  - Enabled system app visibility, selected Calendar, verified the UI count and persisted package value, then cleared the test selection.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): screenshots are posted in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88904#issuecomment-4589598381, with copied live `uiautomator` and app prefs output below as supporting proof:

```text
UI after selecting Calendar:
32:<node index="2" text="Selected Apps" ... bounds="[100,617][945,667]" />
54:<node index="0" text="App Filter" ... bounds="[95,1470][271,1524]" />
55:<node index="1" text="1 app blocked from forwarding." ... bounds="[95,1550][607,1600]" />
57:<node index="0" text="Close App Picker" ... bounds="[397,1664][684,1714]" />
60:<node index="0" text="Search apps" ... bounds="[124,1799][333,1849]" />
64:<node index="0" text="Show System Apps" ... bounds="[195,1912][516,1962]" />
75:<node index="1" text="Calendar" ... bounds="[198,2126][351,2176]" />
76:<node index="2" text="com.google.android.calendar" ... bounds="[198,2179][636,2224]" />
77:<node ... checkable="true" checked="true" clickable="true" ... bounds="[848,2112][985,2238]">

Stored notification package value:
6:    <string name="notifications.forwarding.packages">["com.google.android.calendar"]</string>
9:    <boolean name="notifications.forwarding.enabled" value="false" />

UI after clearing Calendar:
32:<node index="2" text="Selected Apps" ... />
33:<node index="3" text="0" ... />
54:<node index="0" text="App Filter" ... />
55:<node index="1" text="No apps blocked. Apps can forward unless you add blocks." ... />
75:<node index="1" text="Calendar" ... />
76:<node index="2" text="com.google.android.calendar" ... />
77:<node ... checkable="true" checked="false" clickable="true" ...>

Stored notification package value after cleanup:
6:    <string name="notifications.forwarding.packages">[]</string>
9:    <boolean name="notifications.forwarding.enabled" value="false" />
```

- Observed result after fix: users can now explicitly add or remove apps for the notification Blocklist/Allowlist from the current settings screen, and the existing package storage value changes only when a package switch is toggled.
- What was not tested: a physical Android phone, Play flavor build, and background notification delivery from a real third-party app after changing the filter.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: emulator proof covers the settings UI and package persistence path; it does not prove real notification delivery from another app.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this change, the current notification settings screen only showed forwarding mode plus selected-app count and did not expose an app picker.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `cd apps/android && ./gradlew :app:testThirdPartyDebugUnitTest :app:assembleThirdPartyDebug` failed first because `ANDROID_HOME` / `ANDROID_SDK_ROOT` were not set in the shell environment.
- `cd apps/android && ANDROID_HOME=<local Android SDK> ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=<local Android SDK> ./gradlew :app:testThirdPartyDebugUnitTest :app:assembleThirdPartyDebug` passed after rebasing onto current `upstream/main`.
- `cd apps/android && ANDROID_HOME=<local Android SDK> ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=<local Android SDK> ./gradlew :app:installThirdPartyDebug` passed on the same API 35 emulator used for proof.
- Local emulator smoke passed on head `c491f9ee239d`: install third-party debug build, open notification settings, expand the app picker, toggle system apps, select Calendar, verify persisted package selection, then clear it.
- `codex review --base upstream/main` was attempted, but local Codex auth returned `401 Unauthorized` before a review could complete.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added tests for `filterNotificationAppsForPicker` so selected system packages stay visible when system apps are hidden.
- Added tests for search matching against both app labels and package names.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The current notification settings screen had no user-facing app picker even though notification package filtering mode and selected package count were present.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A. Focused unit coverage was added for the picker filtering behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The notification settings screen now shows an app filter panel and picker controls.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. It writes the same notification forwarding package CSV through the existing view model API.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Notification listener access, Android notification permission, gateway connection, auth, secrets, network, and tool execution behavior are unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Notification filtering UX and storage: avoiding hidden selected packages and avoiding accidental auto-selection.

How is that risk mitigated?

Selected packages stay visible even when system apps are hidden, search covers labels and package names, the picker only changes packages when the user toggles an explicit app switch, and emulator proof now shows both the UI switch state and persisted package value.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Fresh ClawSweeper review, then maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on ClawSweeper to re-check the added copied live proof, plus normal maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper proof feedback from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88904#issuecomment-4589455503 by adding inspectable copied live output for the visible UI and persisted package value.
